mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in real retail sales signals robust consumer demand, which drives corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the forward 20-day return is negative in more than 45% of instances when RSAFS rises month-over-month over any 2-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 4.5%, it signals a restrictive monetary policy stance that increases the cost of capital and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative forward S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day forward return following FEDFUNDS > 4.5% is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) over the sample, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent positive growth in both nonfarm payroll employment and real retail sales indicates expansion in labor income and consumer spending, mutually reinforcing economic momentum that boosts S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: If the combined signal (e.g., both above median month-over-month growth) does not yield a positive average forward return significantly above zero (p > 0.10), the interaction hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where the 12-month CPI inflation rate exceeds 3%, further acceleration in CPI signals demand overheating and an increased likelihood of Fed tightening, which depresses equity prices over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the forward 20-day return is not negative on average during periods when inflation is above 3% and accelerating month-over-month, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and broader economic activity, which is positively correlated with corporate earnings and supports higher equity valuations over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the rank correlation between INDPRO growth and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns is less than 0.05 over any 5-year rolling period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.5 percentage points over a three-month period signals a rapid deterioration in labor market conditions, raising recession fears and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases more than 0.5pp over 3 months
failure_condition: If the average 20-day return after the trigger is not negative or if the hit rate of negative returns is less than 55% over the sample, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts experience a monthly decline exceeding 5% simultaneously with a rise in the federal funds rate, it indicates an adverse credit tightening that constrains housing demand and broader economic growth, leading to negative equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the joint occurrence (HOUST decline >5% m/m and FEDFUNDS rise >0.25% m/m) yields a positive average forward return or insufficiently negative (p > 0.10), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of full employment defined by an unemployment rate below 4%, continued additions to nonfarm payrolls signal labor market overheating and potential wage inflation that compresses corporate profit margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprice this risk.
failure_condition: If the average 5-day forward return following a positive PAYEMS surprise (above consensus) when UNRATE is below 4% is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10), the hypothesis is rejected.
